evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Endpoints api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast and geocoding-api.open-meteo.com/v1/search with params hourly=temperature_2m, past_days, forecast_days, timezone=auto are consistent and match well-known Open-Meteo API surface. Internally consistent with no version claim; docs_unavailable applies.
evidence.executability: Code blocks are syntactically valid Python/JSX. However backend code uses relative imports (from .config) without showing package structure (no __init__.py or directory layout), pydantic BaseSettings import is from pydantic (v1) but FastAPI standard installs pydantic v2 where BaseSettings moved to pydantic_settings — would cause ImportError. Multiple fix points needed.
evidence.completeness: (a) no API keys needed - acknowledged; (b) install bash blocks present; (c) .env template present (VITE_API_BASE_URL, backend env); (d) backend code present; (e) frontend code present; (f) test commands shown but no explicit single 'run tests' bash block under a Test header. Mostly complete but split across many sections.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Citations are bare bracketed numbers like [1][6][7] with no URLs. No official docs URLs (open-meteo.com/en/docs, fastapi.tiangolo.com) are explicitly written. Fails canonical URL requirement.

rubric_notes: Very long, verbose prose-heavy playbook. Pydantic v1 BaseSettings is a likely runtime trap on fresh installs. Citations are numeric refs without URLs, severely hurting anchoring.
